summary: Static code review of password complexity policy feature (no dotnet SDK available). All 9 acceptance checks pass: new LocalizedPasswordComplexityAttribute is correctly implemented (ValidationAttribute + IClientModelValidator, SpecialChars="!?@#$%^&*", returns true on empty/null to defer to LocalizedRequired, enforces min 8 / upper / lower / digit / special), RegisterViewModel.Parola and ResetPasswordViewModel.Parola both use [LocalizedPasswordComplexity] + [StringLength(100)] with no LocalizedStringLength, LoginViewModel.Parola has only LocalizedRequired (legacy 6-char passwords still work), all 6 new i18n keys present in every one of the 7 language blocks (en/ro/fr/es/de/it/pt) with equal key counts (parity holds), password-complexity.js registers jQuery Validate 'pwdcomplex' method + 7-param unobtrusive adapter, scans on DOMContentLoaded for input[type=password][data-val-pwdcomplex-min], adds Bootstrap 5 popover with trigger:'click', live feedback panel toggling .ok/.fail classes, all texts from data-* attributes, DEFAULT_SPECIAL matches C#, password-complexity.css includes `white-space: pre-line` for both .field-validation-error and .text-danger, both Home/Index.cshtml (unified B2C+B2B) and Account/ResetPassword.cshtml include CSS + JS, no changes to LoginViewModel/login flow, brace balance = 0 in all touched C# files.
critical_code_review_comments: Consistency: request says each language block should have 1004 keys; actual count is 1005 per block (all 7 blocks equal — parity holds, so the exact number is a doc mismatch, not a bug).

Home/Index.cshtml loads jQuery + jquery-validation + unobtrusive from CDN inside @section Scripts. If _Layout.cshtml also loads them, they may be loaded twice; harmless but worth verifying to avoid double-registration of validators.

password-complexity.js: on submit the jQuery Validate error message is written into a <span asp-validation-for> which typically has class `text-danger small`. CSS applies `white-space: pre-line` to .text-danger — good. However the client-side error span is a <span> (inline). Newlines will render but the span stays inline; consider adding `display:block` in CSS to guarantee the multi-line list stacks under the input on all browsers (currently relies on default flow).
escapeHtml() intentionally does not escape the apostrophe (') — safe here because attribute values use double quotes, but note for future maintainers.

In LocalizedPasswordComplexityAttribute.AddValidation, the composed FormatErrorMessage() is written to data-val-pwdcomplex, but the JS adapter overwrites options.messages['pwdcomplex'] with its own composition — the server-attribute value is effectively unused on the client. Not a bug (both compositions are identical), but slightly wasteful; leave as-is for defense-in-depth.

SpecialChars set '!?@#$%^&*' is duplicated in 3 places (C# const, JS DEFAULT_SPECIAL, and 7 i18n strings). Attribute already emits data-val-pwdcomplex-specialset so JS uses server value at runtime — good; the i18n strings are the only hard-coded copies and were verified to match in all 7 languages.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sandbox has no dotnet SDK — static review only. User runtime-tests locally on Windows/VS2026 after git pull. Feature verified: LocalizedPasswordComplexityAttribute (Models/LocalizedAttributes.cs L70-131), applied to RegisterViewModel.Parola (L14) and ResetPasswordViewModel.Parola (L83), NOT applied to LoginViewModel (L51-62) so legacy passwords still work. i18n keys at Loc.cs lines 1089-1094 (en), 2174-2179 (ro), 3238-3243 (fr), 4302-4307 (es), 5366-5371 (de), 6381-6386 (it), 7394-7399 (pt). JS/CSS at wwwroot/js/password-complexity.js + wwwroot/css/password-complexity.css. Both views (Home/Index.cshtml, Account/ResetPassword.cshtml) include both assets.
